name: Caraway Vinegar
recipeIngredient: 1 cup packed fresh sage leaves (for garnish)
recipeInstructions:
1. Place the sage leaves in a clean quart-sized glass jar.
2. Bruise the sage leaves with a wooden spoon.
3. Add the caraway seeds, vinegar, and water to the jar.
4. Cover the jar tightly and store it in a cool, dark place for 4 days to 2 weeks.
5. Strain the mixture through a fine sieve into a glass pitcher, discarding the solids.
6. Pour the strained liquid into two clean half-pint glass jars.
7. Add the sage sprigs to each jar.
8. Seal the jars tightly with lids.
Once steeped, this vinegar can keep for several months. (Garnishes and other solids left in the jar may discolor or break down.)
